samples.xml_description:
<module id="div_8bit">
    <description>Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. The design supports both signed and unsigned division operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst">The reset signal to initialize or reset the module.</input>
        <input id="sign">1-bit indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">1-bit indicates that a valid operation request is present.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">1-bit indicates that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit the output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. The design supports both signed and unsigned division operations.</implementation>
    <parameter_description>
        <state name="IDLE" value="2'b00"/>
        <state name="DIVIDE" value="2'b01"/>
        <state name="DONE" value="2'b10"/>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="state" type="2-bit" initial_value="IDLE">State variable for controlling the division process.</reg>
        <reg id="count" type="3-bit" initial_value="0">Count variable for tracking the number of iterations.</reg>
        <reg id="remainder" type="8-bit" initial_value="0">Register to hold the current remainder during division.</reg>
        <reg id="quotient" type="8-bit" initial_value="0">Register to hold the current quotient during division.</reg>
        <reg id="dividend_reg" type="8-bit" initial_value="0">Register to store the dividend input.</reg>
        <reg id="divisor_reg" type="8-bit" initial_value="0">Register to store the divisor input.</reg>
        <wire id="sub_result" type="9-bit"/> // Result of subtraction
        <wire id="carry_out" type="1-bit"/> // Carry out from subtraction
        <always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin
            if (rst) begin
                state <= IDLE;
                count <= 0;
                remainder <= 0;
                quotient <= 0;
                dividend_reg <= 0;
                divisor_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                case (state)
                    IDLE: begin
                        if (opn_valid && !res_valid) begin
                            dividend_reg <= dividend;
                            divisor_reg <= divisor;
                            remainder <= {1'b0, dividend}; // Shift dividend left by 1
                            state <= DIVIDE;
                        end
                    end
                    
                    DIVIDE: begin
                        if (count == 8) begin
                            state <= DONE;
                        end else begin
                            // Perform subtraction
                            sub_result = remainder + {1'b0, ~divisor_reg} + 1;
                            carry_out = sub_result[8]; // Check carry out
                            
                            // Update remainder and quotient
                            remainder <= {sub_result[7:0], carry_out};
                            quotient <= {quotient[6:0], carry_out};
                            count <= count + 1;
                        end
                    end
                    
                    DONE: begin
                        state <= IDLE;
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <task>
        <step>On receiving a positive edge of the clock, check if the reset signal is active. If so, reset all registers and outputs to their initial values.</step>
        <step>When opn_valid is high and res_valid is low, save the inputs dividend and divisor into respective registers and initialize the shift register SR with the absolute value of the dividend shifted left by one bit. Set NEG_DIVISOR to the negated absolute value of the divisor. Set the counter cnt to 1 and start_cnt to 1 to begin the division process.</step>
        <step>During the division process, if start_cnt is high, check if the counter cnt has reached 8 (indicating completion). If so, clear cnt and start_cnt, update the shift register SR with the final remainder and quotient, and transition back to IDLE state. Otherwise, increment the counter cnt and update the shift register SR based on the subtraction result, performing the necessary shifts and updates accordingly.</step>
        <step>After completing the division process, manage the validity of the result. When res_valid is high and res_ready is also high, set res_valid to 0. Otherwise, if res_valid is high, set res_valid to 1. This ensures that the result is marked as valid until it is consumed by the system.</step>
    </task>
</module>
